description: Run shell commands in a sandboxed session, index their output, and return only query-matched excerpts — keeping raw output out of agent context windows

specs.description: batch_execute runs all commands and returns a session handle with summary stats
specs.given: A list of valid shell commands and a working directory path
specs.then: Returns a BatchSession { session_id: <uuid>, commands_run: N, total_lines: M, exit_codes: [...] } without emitting raw output to the caller

specs.description: search returns lines matching the query from a valid session
specs.given: A BatchSession whose indexed output contains lines matching 'error[E'
specs.then: Returns Vec<SearchResult> with line, line_number, command, and score >= 0.9 for exact matches

specs.description: Commands with non-zero exit codes still have their output indexed
specs.given: A command that exits with code 1 and emits stderr output
specs.then: Session is created, exit_codes contains [1], stderr output is indexed and searchable

domain_conventions.stderr_handling: stderr and stdout are both captured and indexed; they are merged per line in emission order

specs.description: search with no matches returns an empty result set, not an error
specs.given: A valid session whose output does not contain the string 'xyzzy_notfound'
specs.then: Returns Ok(vec![]) — empty Vec, no error

specs.description: Sessions are evicted from memory after their TTL expires
specs.given: A session created more than 10 minutes ago with no subsequent search calls

domain_conventions.eviction: background task sweeps expired sessions every 60 seconds

specs.description: Raw command output does NOT appear in the return value of batch_execute
specs.given: A command that produces 500 lines of output
specs.then: The returned BatchSession contains only metadata (counts, exit codes) — no raw lines

domain_conventions.context_protection: This is the core invariant: output is indexed server-side, never returned wholesale to callers

specs.description: hex_batch_execute MCP tool returns a session summary suitable for agent use
specs.given: An MCP client calls hex_batch_execute with commands=['cargo check'] and working_dir='/project'
specs.when: The MCP server processes the tool call
specs.then: Returns JSON { session_id, commands_run, total_lines, exit_codes } — not the raw cargo output

specs.description: hex_batch_search MCP tool returns matched excerpts with surrounding context lines
specs.then: Returns JSON array of { command, line_number, line, score } sorted by score descending, max 50 results

domain_conventions.context_lines: 0 by default; callers may request ±N context lines via context_lines param

specs.description: Memory cap is enforced — sessions beyond HEX_CMD_SESSION_MAX_MB are rejected
specs.given: The adapter is at its configured memory limit (default 500MB of indexed output)
specs.when: batch_execute is called for a new session
specs.then: Returns Err(CommandSessionError::CapacityExceeded) and does not execute commands

specs.description: Commands exceeding the execution timeout are terminated and flagged
specs.given: A command that does not exit within 60 seconds
specs.then: The command is killed, exit_code is -1, and the session contains output captured up to the timeout point
